text: The surgeon performs adhesiolysis on the left lobe of the liver, exposing the gastro-jejunostomy. The gastrogenostomy is transected using purple staples. Gastric continuity is restored through a linear staple side-to-side anastomosis between the proximal gastric pouch and the excluded stomach. An anisogastric tube is inserted into the gastric pouch and excluded stomach to maintain the anastomosis patency. The stapler entry hole is closed with an unabsorbable barbed suture.

The surgeon resects the omega-loop jejunum and performs a side-to-side anastomosis using staples to prevent intestinal stenosis. Subsequently, the mesenteric defect is closed with a running, non-absorbable suture.

The surgeon assesses anastomotic leakage by injecting methylene blue-stained saline via an orogastric tube, reinforcing the gastrogastric anastomosis with biological glue. The contrast upper gastrointestinal study shows contrast draining well into the excluded stomach.
